program.targetPhase1Design: Single ascending dose followed by short multiple ascending dose in healthy volunteers, with PK, food-effect, and CSF-enabled biomarker exploration if exposure supports it.
program.targetIndStrategy: Build an IND package around oral exposure, CNS penetration, early efficacy plausibility, and a coherent nonclinical safety narrative that supports first-in-human dose escalation.

nodes.title: Rodent PK / brain exposure
nodes.objective: Establish whether the lead reaches brain exposure compatible with the intended oral Phase 1 path.
nodes.procedureSummary: Single-dose mouse PK with plasma and brain sampling across 8 hours using the intended formulation.
nodes.successCriteria: Free brain exposure clears the projected efficacious concentration with usable variability and oral tolerability.
nodes.decisionSupported: Supports brain penetration claim and informs early clinical dose framing.
nodes.operationalNotes: Bioanalysis vendor has a provisional slot hold for next week.
nodes.phase1Relevance: The target Phase 1 design assumes oral dosing can achieve CNS-relevant exposure margins.
nodes.indRelevance: Feeds the clinical pharmacology narrative and exposure-driven rationale for dose escalation.

nodes.title: TNF / NF-kB microglia pathway evidence
nodes.summary: Mechanistic evidence node for inflammatory signaling context around the CNS program.

paperSources.label: Demo raw text
paperSources.sourceValue:
Introduction
TNF is known to influence NF-kB signaling.

Results
In a murine inflammatory challenge model, TNF increased NF-kB activation in microglia relative to vehicle control. Figure 2 showed elevated nuclear p65 and increased downstream cytokine expression after TNF stimulation.
